commit.message:
fix: configure workspace members for uv-dynamic-versioning and update Dockerfile to use uv build (#325)

* fix: configure workspace members for uv-dynamic-versioning and update Dockerfile to use uv build

PR #302 unified versioning via uv-dynamic-versioning but the workspace
member packages (codeweaver-daemon, codeweaver-tokenizers) still had
hardcoded version = "0.1.0". The Dockerfile used pip install which
couldn't resolve the root package's exact version pins on the siblings
(e.g. code-weaver-daemon==0.1.3.dev5+g969a8a63 vs installed 0.1.0).

Changes:
- Add uv-dynamic-versioning to workspace member build-system.requires
- Configure [tool.hatch.version] and [tool.uv-dynamic-versioning] in
  both workspace member pyproject.toml files
- Make version dynamic in workspace members (remove hardcoded 0.1.0)
- Update Dockerfile to use uv build --wheel --all-packages for
  consistent VCS-derived versions across all workspace packages
- Update uv.lock to reflect dynamic workspace member versions

to prevent HQL injection in DemographicDao.findByField() which concatenates
both values directly into HQL ORDER BY and WHERE clauses.

bindings) with direct server-side rendering using SxmlMisc.getXmlContent()
to extract xml_content and xml_username, then encode with Encode.forHtml().

refactor: unified-web-skill v3.0 — 6-engine architecture

Complete rewrite integrating 6 web engines under unified MCP interface:

Architecture:
- Engine Protocol with Capability enum (FETCH/SEARCH/INTERACT/STRUCTURED)
- EngineManager with SmartRouter + 5-layer fallback chain
- SiteRegistry: 67+ built-in sites with auto engine matching
- HealthMonitor: per-engine circuit breaker (closed→open→half-open)
- IntentClassifier: 9 query intent types, 130+ keyword patterns
- ResearchPipeline v3: intent→expand→discover→fetch→extract→quality→dedup→save

6 Engines:
- OpenCLI (100+ site adapters, structured data)
- Scrapling (3-tier HTTP: Fetcher→AsyncFetcher→StealthyFetcher)
- Lightpanda (Zig CDP browser, 9x speed, 16x less memory)
- PinchTab (remote browser MCP, session reuse)
- bb-browser (126+ adapters, most capable: FETCH+SEARCH+INTERACT+STRUCTURED)
- CLIBrowser (Rust stealth binary, ultimate fallback)

7 MCP Tools:
- research_and_collect (full pipeline, backward compatible)
- web_fetch (single URL, auto routing)
- web_cli (direct site commands)
- web_interact (browser automation)
- web_search (NEW: multi-engine search)
- web_crawl (NEW: BFS link-following crawl)
- engine_status (NEW: health check)

Tests: 327 passed, 19 skipped, 0 failures
Docs: README.md + docs/architecture.md + docs/api.md + docs/engines.md

commit.message:
fix: set distDir='../.next' on Vercel to match post-build validator path

Vercel's post-build validator (introduced in recent @vercel/next update)
looks for routes-manifest-deterministic.json at /vercel/path0/.next/.
With rootDirectory:frontend, the build runs from /vercel/path0/frontend/
and outputs to frontend/.next — one directory below where the validator
looks. Setting distDir='../.next' when VERCEL=1 redirects the build output
to /vercel/path0/.next/ so the validator finds the file.

Locally VERCEL env var is not set so distDir defaults to .next (normal).

Also squashes the empty trigger commit into this real fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace 1-hour JWT auth tokens with permanent HMAC autologin codes

Newsletter links now use HMAC-based autologin codes (?ac=<hex>) instead of
Firebase custom tokens (?at=<jwt>). Key improvements:

- Never expires: HMAC code is deterministic, client exchanges it for a fresh
  Firebase token on-demand via Cloud Function API call
- Much shorter URLs: 67 chars (HMAC hex) vs 793 chars (JWT) — well under
  Mailgun's 1000-char click tracking limit
- No Firebase Admin SDK needed at send time: pure HMAC, no async token
  generation per subscriber

Changes:
- scripts/send-newsletter.mjs: Replace getOrCreateCustomToken() with
  generateAutologinCode() using HMAC('autologin:'+email, secret)
- functions/src/newsletterSubscriptionManagement.js: Add exchange_auth_code
  action that validates HMAC and returns fresh createCustomToken()
- functions/index.js: Support JSON responses from exchange_auth_code
- functions/src/newsletterConfirmationEmail.js: Remove JWT from confirmation
  URLs (Cloud Function already returns token in API response)
- App.tsx: Auto-login useEffect exchanges ac code via API; action handler
  supports ac for unsubscribe/resubscribe; skip auto-login for confirm action
- services/newsletterSubscribers.ts: Add exchangeNewsletterAuthCode()
- services/authService.ts: Check ac param in shouldWaitForAuth()
- build-plugins/constants.ts: Add ac to SPA redirect script

Backwards compatible: legacy at/authToken params still work for old emails.

commit.message:
watchdog: distinguish RPC busy from de-registration; add audit sections 14.11-14.12

- RPC timeout no longer immediately counts as de-registration.
  If timed has logged within DAEMON_ACTIVE_SECS (default 90s), the
  daemon is alive but busy (fork resolution, reconnection storm).
  Busy timeouts increment rpc_busy_streak; only after RPC_BUSY_MAX
  (default 10 consecutive polls, ~90s) without log activity does it
  escalate to a fail_streak increment and eventual restart.
- When busy: logs '⏳ RPC timeout — daemon is alive and logging
  (busy_streak: N/10); NOT counting as de-registration'
- When silent AND unresponsive: logs 'RPC call failed — daemon has
  NOT logged in 90s' and escalates normally.
- RPC_TIMEOUT default: 3s → 8s (more headroom during load spikes)
- FAIL_THRESHOLD default: 2 → 3 (confirmed not-active before restart)
- daemon_recently_active() function added (journalctl short-unix parse)
- rpc_busy_streak state variable + reset on RPC success
- Restart log message: 'detected' → 'confirmed' to reduce false alarm tone
- New --rpc-busy-max N and --daemon-active-secs N CLI flags
- docs: add sections 14.11 (tokio starvation via reconnection storm)
  and 14.12 (watchdog false-restart on RPC timeout) to security audit;
  add three new rows to summary attack surface table

Fixes the pattern seen Apr 8 2026: restarts #12-17 at 10-min intervals
triggered by RPC starvation from the fork-resolution busy-loop.

fix(smoketest): prevent SIGPIPE from trivy killing script via EXIT trap

trivy --version outputs many lines; head -1 closes the pipe early causing
trivy to get SIGPIPE (exit 141). With set -eo pipefail this non-zero exit
fires the EXIT trap inside the $() subshell which calls cleanup/exit 0,
terminating the whole script. Add '|| true' to suppress the SIGPIPE.

commit.message:
fix: restore turbopack.root in next.config.ts to fix Vercel build

Without turbopack.root, Vercel's Turbopack build could not correctly anchor
its project root to frontend/. This caused the post-build validation step to
look for routes-manifest-deterministic.json at /vercel/path0/.next/ (repo
root) instead of /vercel/path0/frontend/.next/ (correct project root).

The turbopack.root removal in 75045806 was thought to fix Railway, but the
actual Railway fix was the lightningcss + tailwind oxide optionalDependencies
pins. Restoring turbopack.root is safe — Railway uses the native package fix,
Vercel uses the correct turbopack root anchor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
